ETLDelphi 0.1.0
  • Reference
  • Articles
    • delphi100k: Source Data and OMOP CDM Mapping
    • Delphi to OMOP CDM ETL: Workflow and Design Decisions
    • How to Run the ETL
    • run_etl: Detailed Steps and Mechanics

    How to Run the ETL

    ETLDelphi

    2026-02-10

    how_to_run.Rmd

    Overview

    ETLDelphi runs a sequenced set of SQL scripts that transform the Delphi/Delfi source schema into OMOP CDM v5.4 tables in DuckDB. This vignette explains how to prepare your database and run the ETL from R.

    Prerequisites

    1. DuckDB database with two schemas

    • src — Source tables (Delphi schema): enrollment, encounter, provider, death, problem, medication_orders, medication_fulfillment, current_medications, immunization, lab_orders, lab_results, vital_sign, allergy, therapy_orders, therapy_actions. Create them using the Delphi DDL (e.g. delphi_ddl.sql) under schema src.
    • cdm — OMOP CDM v5.4 tables and vocabulary tables. Create from the official OMOP DuckDB DDL (e.g. OMOPCDM_duckdb_5.4_ddl.sql) with schema name cdm, and load vocabulary (concept, concept_relationship, source_to_concept_map, etc.) as needed for mapping.

    2. Source data loaded into src

    Load your CSV (or other) data into src.* using DuckDB’s read_csv_auto or R’s DBI::dbWriteTable(). The ETL reads only from src and writes to stg and cdm.

    3. R packages

    Install and load:

    • DBI, duckdb — database connection and DuckDB driver
    • ETLDelphi — provides run_etl() and run_sql_scripts()

    The package also uses fs, glue, yaml, cli, digest, withr (installed with ETLDelphi).

    Install and load the package

    From the package root (e.g. after cloning the repo):

    # Install the package
devtools::install("ETLDelphi")

# Or for development: load without installing
devtools::load_all("ETLDelphi")

    Then in your session:

    library(DBI)
library(duckdb)
library(ETLDelphi)

    Connect to DuckDB

    Open a DBI connection to your DuckDB file (or in-memory database). The same connection is used for the entire ETL.

    # Path to your DuckDB file (or ":memory:" for in-memory)
db_path <- "path/to/your.duckdb"
con <- DBI::dbConnect(duckdb::duckdb(), db_path)

    Ensure src and cdm schemas and data are present before running the ETL.

    Run the ETL

    Full run

    Execute all ETL steps in order (admin → stage → keys → vocab → cdm_core → cdm_clinical → cdm_derived → qc):

    run_etl(con)

    The function uses the default config at inst/extdata/config.yml and the SQL scripts under inst/sql/. Each script is run in lexicographic order; the first failure stops the run and reports the step.

    Dry run (list steps only)

    To see which SQL files would run without executing them:

    run_etl(con, dry_run = TRUE)

    Run a subset of steps

    Use from_step and to_step to start from or stop at a given step. Steps are identified by folder or file name prefix (e.g. "40_cdm_core", "54_load_visit").

    # From staging through CDM core, then stop after visit_occurrence
run_etl(con, from_step = "40_cdm_core", to_step = "54_load_visit")

# Only QC scripts
run_etl(con, from_step = "90_qc")

    Custom config or SQL directory

    • Config: Pass a path to your own YAML or a list override.
    run_etl(con, config_path = "/path/to/my_config.yml")
# Or pass a list (config_path is ignored)
run_etl(con, config = list(schemas = list(src = "src", stg = "stg", cdm = "cdm")))
    • SQL directory: By default the package uses system.file("sql", package = "ETLDelphi"). To use a different directory:
    run_etl(con, sql_dir = "/path/to/sql")

    Execution order

    The runner executes .sql files under inst/sql/ in lexicographic order:

    Order Folder Purpose
    1 00_admin Create stg schema, ETL log tables, clear staging tables
    2 10_stage Stage all source tables and reject tables
    3 20_keys Build key maps: person, provider, visit, location, care_site
    4 30_vocab Build concept maps: gender, race, visit type, drug, LOINC, units, condition, vitals, allergy, therapy
    5 40_cdm_core Load location, care_site, provider, person, visit_occurrence, note, death
    6 50_cdm_clinical Load condition, drug_exposure, measurement, observation (allergy), procedure (therapy)
    7 60_cdm_derived Load observation_period
    8 90_qc Row counts, mapping coverage, referential integrity, parse failures, required fields

    Scripts use placeholders @cdmDatabaseSchema, {cdm}, {stg}, {src} which are replaced with the schema names from config (default: cdm, stg, src).

    Configuration

    The default config is inst/extdata/config.yml. Important options:

    • schemas: src, stg, cdm — must match your database.
    • concept_id_unknown: Sentinel for unmapped concepts (default 0).
    • date_formats: Used for parsing dates in staging (DuckDB formats).
    • type_concept_ids: OMOP type concept IDs for visits, conditions, drugs, measurements, etc.
    • prefer_fulfillment: If true, medication orders with matching fulfillment can be excluded (logic can be extended in the drug scripts).

    Edit this file or pass config_path / config to run_etl() to override.

    After the run

    ETL logs (if admin step ran)

    • stg.etl_run_log — One row per ETL run (run_id, started_at, ended_at, status).
    • stg.etl_step_log — One row per script (run_id, step_name, file_name, started_at, ended_at, status, rows_affected, notes).

    QC tables (after 90_qc)

    • stg.qc_row_counts — Row counts per staging and CDM table.
    • stg.qc_mapping_coverage — Proportion of unmapped concepts (e.g. concept_id = 0) per clinical table.
    • stg.qc_orphans — Referential integrity (e.g. visit_occurrence.person_id not in person).
    • stg.qc_parse_failures — Counts per stg.reject_* table.
    • stg.qc_required_fields — NOT NULL / required-field violations.

    Query these after each run to validate data quality and mapping coverage.

    Disconnect

    When finished:

    DBI::dbDisconnect(con, shutdown = TRUE)

    Summary

    1. Prepare DuckDB with src (Delphi data) and cdm (OMOP DDL + vocabulary).
    2. Load the package and connect with DBI::dbConnect(duckdb::duckdb(), ...).
    3. Run the ETL with run_etl(con); use dry_run = TRUE or from_step/to_step as needed.
    4. Optionally override config or SQL directory via config_path, config, or sql_dir.
    5. After the run, check stg.etl_*_log and stg.qc_* tables for status and data quality.

    For mapping tables, vocabulary setup, and known limitations, see the package README and function help (?run_etl, ?run_sql_scripts).

    Developed by Adam Black.

    Site built with pkgdown 2.2.0.
